title: Bending-related faulting of the incoming oceanic plate and its effect on lithospheric hydration and seismicity: A passive and active seismological study offshore Maule, Chile
abstract: New active and passive seismological data offshore Maule, Chile, examine the dependency between incoming plate structure, bending-related faulting, lithospheric hydration, and outer rise seismic activity. A 2D Poisson's ratio distribution is derived from P- and S-wave seismic wide-angle data collected at the trench-outer rise. High values of Poisson's ratio in the uppermost mantle suggest that the oceanic lithosphere is highly hydrated due to water infiltration through bending-related normal faults outcropping at the seafloor between 34°S and 35°S.

location.description: Outer rise of the incoming Nazca plate offshore Maule (34°–35°S); active and passive OBS study

seismic_lines.name: OBS P/S-wave wide-angle profile — Maule outer rise 34°–35°S

data.name: OBS P- and S-Wave Wide-Angle Data — Maule Outer Rise 34°–35°S
data.description: Active-source OBS wide-angle P- and S-wave data from the Maule outer rise (34°–35°S); 2D Poisson's ratio model reveals high serpentinization via bending-related normal faults; combined active and passive seismic study

data.name: Bathymetry — Maule Outer Rise 34°–35°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry used to map bending-related fault scarps at the outer rise offshore Maule

_data_note: Journal of Geodynamics paper; behind paywall. Post-Maule cruise data; specific cruise not confirmed.

processing.summary: Active-source P- and S-wave wide-angle data and passive aftershock recordings from OBS instruments at the Maule outer rise (34°–35°S) are processed to derive 2D Vp, Vs and Poisson's ratio models. High Poisson's ratio values in the uppermost mantle indicate highly serpentinized lithosphere (>10%) via bending-related normal fault pathways. Combined active-passive study complementing Moscoso & Contreras-Reyes 2012.
processing.notes: Companion study to Moscoso & Contreras-Reyes 2012; uses both active and passive OBS data

analysis_notes: Journal of Geodynamics paper; behind paywall. Specific cruise not confirmed. Data repository unknown.
